message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Adding process_priority parameter to click.edit()/Editor class for CPU priority control of editor subprocess

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Adding multi-file support to click.edit() - editing edit_file->edit_files and termui.edit() signature

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Done: renamed Editor.edit_file(filename) to Editor.edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]); termui.edit() filename param now accepts str | Iterable[str] | None with overloads; edit() joins quoted filenames with subprocess.Popen. Please rebase your process_priority param on top of edit_files() signature (filenames: Iterable[str], not filename: str).

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: In my sandbox Editor.edit_file (singular) is unchanged/not renamed to edit_files - our repo copies appear isolated. Implemented process_priority as optional Editor ctor param + termui.edit() kwarg, applied via subprocess.Popen creationflags (Windows) or preexec_fn os.nice (Unix), with graceful fallback to plain Popen on failure. No edit_files signature present on my end to rebase onto.
